title: gratiola pilosa
intro: Gratiola pilosa is a perennial plant native to North America, also known as the "slippery foxglove," belonging to the family Plantaginaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Gratiola pilosa include its ability to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health. Gratiola pilosa is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be consumed as a dried herb or added to salves and creams for topical use.
